The expansion targets the silicon photonics that move data between GPUs, a bottleneck Nvidia is racing to shore up as cluster sizes grow and copper links hit distance and bandwidth limits.

title: Vibe-decoding the White House-Anthropic fight over Fable
blurb: The White House imposed export controls on Anthropic's Mythos 5 and Fable 5 models on Friday, barring foreign governments and nationals from access and forcing Anthropic to cut off those users. The Verge argues the chaotic rollout reflects competing Trump-administration factions leaking rival narratives, leaving it unclear who drove the decision or who would have owned the resulting cybersecurity risk.

title: Samsung Display just showed why XR’s future may come down to better tiny screens
blurb: Samsung Display introduced a 1.3-inch RGB OLEDoS panel rated at 40,000 nits, targeting the brightness and efficiency demands of XR headsets and AR smart glasses. The technology builds OLED directly on a wafer and drops the color filter used in white OLEDoS, which Samsung says simplifies manufacturing and could enable thinner devices, though the company shared no product timelines, pricing, or customer names.

title: Als eine US-Drohne versagt, überrascht ein Startup aus München im Kampf gegen russische Störsender
blurb: Munich defense startup Helsing's HX-2 attack drone hit its target in 15 of 17 flights during the US-led "Flytrap 5" exercise in Lithuania, where heavy electronic jamming downed many other drones. US soldiers also repurposed the AI-guided system for reconnaissance after an American surveillance drone failed under the same interference, an unusual case of US forces leaning on European autonomous hardware.

title: China pitches free AI for the developing world as the G7 debates who gets access to American models
blurb: China's top diplomat Wang Yi called for a global AI cooperation organization offering cheap or free open-weight models to the developing world, as the G7 discussed restricting US models to "trusted partners." The split is now structural: Washington's frontier systems are subscription-only and export-controlled, while DeepSeek, Qwen, and other downloadable Chinese models court a Global South that the G7 framework never consulted.

title: Ten months later, the $100 Google Home Speaker is finally available for preorder
blurb: Google's $100 Gemini-powered Home speaker opens for preorder and ships June 25, with the AI assistant as the real selling point over sound.

title: Google’s updated migration tool takes the pain out of iPhone to Android switching
blurb: Google rebuilt Android Switch, its iOS-to-Android migration tool, around a wireless-first transfer that no longer needs cables, adapters, or a separate app and is built directly into device setup. Working with Apple, the revamp expands supported data to passwords, passkeys, Wi-Fi credentials, encrypted RCS and iMessage threads, and Google Account and eSIM transfers, plus new APIs that let developers preserve in-app data across platforms.

title: The Secret to Marathon-Winning Humanoid Robots
blurb: The Honor Lightning humanoid ran a half-marathon in 50:26, and this analysis traces its win to optimal gear-ratio choices rather than novel technology.

title: 144 Mastra npm Packages Compromised via Hijacked Contributor Account
blurb: Attackers hijacked a former Mastra contributor's npm account to publish 144 malicious packages carrying a crypto-stealing trojan via a postinstall hook.

title: Predicting model behavior before release by simulating deployment
blurb: OpenAI introduced Deployment Simulation, a method that replays prior conversations in a privacy-preserving way against a candidate model to estimate how it will behave before release. Across multiple GPT-5-series Thinking deployments, it improved estimates of undesired behavior rates, surfaced novel forms of misalignment, and reduced the risk that models could tell they were being tested.

title: Qwen-Robot Suite: A Foundation Model Suite for Physical World Intelligence
blurb: Alibaba's Qwen team released Qwen-Robot Suite, a family of foundation models aimed at physical world intelligence and robotic control. The suite extends Qwen's vision-language work into embodied settings, targeting perception, planning, and action so robots can interpret their surroundings and execute multi-step tasks in the real world.

title: Z.ai’s open-weights GLM-5.2 beats GPT-5.5 on multiple long-horizon coding benchmarks for 1/6th the cost
blurb: Z.ai released GLM-5.2, a 753B-parameter open-weights model under an MIT license that beats GPT-5.5 on several long-horizon coding benchmarks at roughly one-sixth the cost. It ships with a 1M-token context window, $12.60-per-month enterprise tiers, and an IndexShare architecture that reuses one indexer across every four sparse attention layers to cut per-token compute by 2.9x.

title: Amazon S3 annotations: attach rich, queryable context directly to your objects
blurb: Amazon S3 introduced annotations, a metadata capability that lets you attach up to 1,000 named annotations per object, each up to 1 MB and totaling 1 GB, in JSON, XML, YAML, or plain text. Annotations can be modified or deleted without rewriting objects, travel with objects during copy and replication, and flow into managed tables that are queryable with Amazon Athena when S3 Metadata is enabled.

title: GLM-5.2 is the new leading open weights model on Artificial Analysis
blurb: Z.ai released GLM-5.2, which scores 51 on Artificial Analysis's Intelligence Index v4.1 to become the leading open weights model, ahead of MiniMax-M3 and DeepSeek V4 Pro (both 44). The MIT-licensed model keeps GLM-5.1's 744B total / 40B active parameters but extends the context window to 1M tokens, with first-party pricing at $1.4/$4.4 per 1M input/output tokens.

title: SpaceX to buy Cursor AI coding agent operator Anysphere for $60B
blurb: SpaceX agreed to acquire Anysphere, the company behind the Cursor AI coding agent, for $60B. The deal would fold one of the most widely used AI coding tools into Elon Musk's space and satellite empire, signaling SpaceX's push to build out internal AI software capabilities and developer infrastructure.

title: MolmoMotion: Language-guided 3D motion forecasting
blurb: MolmoMotion is a language-guided model from Allen AI that predicts the future 3D trajectories of object points given a video frame, query points, and a written action description, outperforming existing motion forecasting methods. Alongside the model weights, the team released MolmoMotion-1M, a dataset of 3D point trajectories paired with action descriptions drawn from 1.16M videos, and PointMotionBench, a human-validated benchmark of 2.7K clips.

title: Google Gemini co-lead Noam Shazeer leaves for OpenAI
blurb: Noam Shazeer, Google's VP of engineering and a co-lead of its Gemini models, announced he is leaving to join OpenAI, less than two years after returning to Google in its Character.AI partnership. The exit lands weeks after Google shipped Gemini 3.5 Flash and the Gemini Spark agent at I/O, and as OpenAI moves toward a confidentially filed IPO.

title: OpenAI prepares major ChatGPT voice upgrade with GPT-Bidi-1
blurb: OpenAI is preparing a major ChatGPT voice upgrade built around a next-generation audio model tentatively named GPT-Bidi-1, designed to listen and speak simultaneously, absorb interruptions, and adjust mid-sentence. The bidirectional model would add High, Medium, and Instant intelligence tiers mirroring the text side, closing a gap that left voice on an older stack while text reached the GPT-5.5 generation.

title: The State of Fable, The Jailbreak Problem, SpaceX Acquires Cursor
blurb: Ben Thompson argues that while the administration is very likely wrong in its assessment of Fable, the burden of proof still falls on Anthropic to demonstrate otherwise. The piece ties that question to the broader jailbreak problem, contending that responsibility for model misuse and the perception of it ultimately rests with the labs building the systems, not the regulators reacting to them.

title: Context engineering is the new analytics engineering skill: a practical guide for dbt users
blurb: Analytics engineers already practice context engineering through dbt metric definitions, model descriptions, and schema contracts that make business logic machine-readable.

title: New research shows how AMIE, our medical AI, could help manage health conditions.
blurb: Google published research in Nature showing that AMIE, its medical reasoning AI, can extend from one-off diagnosis to long-term disease management by cross-referencing drug formularies and clinical guidelines. In a blinded study with patient actors, AMIE matched 21 primary care physicians on overall management reasoning and scored higher on plan preciseness and guideline alignment.

title: A near-autonomous AI chemist improves a challenging reaction in medicinal chemistry
blurb: OpenAI connected GPT-5.4 to Molecule.one's Maria, an agentic chemistry system tied to a high-throughput lab, and tasked it with improving Chan-Lam coupling, a reaction used to form carbon-nitrogen bonds. The model identified primary sulfonamides as a hard substrate class and proposed mild oxidants like TEMPO, lifting mean yield from 16.6% to 25.2% across two experimental cycles.

title: From the Hugging Face Hub to robot hardware with Strands Agents and LeRobot
blurb: Strands Robots is an open source SDK from AWS that exposes robot abstractions, simulation, and the LeRobot stack as composable AgentTools, letting you record demonstrations, train, run policy inference, and coordinate a fleet from a single Strands agent. The integration stays thin, keeping LeRobot's dataset format intact while the same agent code moves from simulation to a physical SO-101 with one keyword argument change.

title: Qualcomm said to be circling AI chip biz Tenstorrent in $10B RISC-V power play
blurb: Qualcomm is reportedly in talks to acquire AI chip startup Tenstorrent in a deal valued at around $10B. The move would mark a significant bet on RISC-V, the open instruction set architecture, and bring in Tenstorrent's AI accelerators and engineering talent led by chip designer Jim Keller.

title: Z.ai pitches GLM-5.2 for long-running software engineering tasks
blurb: Z.ai released GLM-5.2, an MIT-licensed open-source model built for long-running software engineering tasks across repository-scale codebases. The model pairs a one million-token context window with IndexShare, a technique that cuts per-token compute by 2.9x at full context, and Z.ai says it trails Claude Opus 4.8 by 1% on the FrontierSWE long-horizon coding benchmark.

title: Introducing LifeSciBench
blurb: OpenAI released LifeSciBench, an expert-written benchmark of 750 tasks spanning seven workflows and seven biological domains, designed to test whether agentic AI can support realistic life science research rather than answer isolated biology questions. Each task pairs a scientific prompt and relevant artifacts with a free-response answer graded against expert-authored rubrics totaling 19,020 criteria, contributed by 173 scientists and 453 expert reviewers.

title: A robot is sprinting towards you. Do you want it running on Claude or Grok?
blurb: Jacky Liang dropped eleven LLMs into a 2D battle royale and ran 30 games to test whether standard benchmarks predict real agent behavior. Grok 4.1 Fast won 13 games at $0.97 per win, 27x cheaper than runner-up Claude Sonnet 4.6, while GPT 5.4 led on kills but placed second and three models won nothing. The takeaway is that winning correlated with neither cost nor benchmark rank, and Claude's cooperative tendencies make it better suited for most real deployments even though they lose battle royales.

title: PhantomSkill: Malicious Code Injection in Agent Skill Ecosystems
blurb: PhantomSkill is an attack framework that hides malicious behavior in an agent skill's auxiliary resources rather than its textual description, exposing a new supply-chain attack surface for LLM coding agents. Its core technique, VulMask, rewrites overt malicious scripts into vulnerability-shaped code that activates only under attacker-controlled triggers, evading automated reviewers while preserving benign utility.

title: AI coding agents taught robots how to install GPUs and cut zip ties
blurb: Researchers at NVIDIA's GEAR lab, Carnegie Mellon, and UC Berkeley released ENPIRE, an agent harness that lets AI coding agents autonomously direct robot training, teaching robotic arms to cut zip ties and insert GPUs into motherboard sockets. The framework was tested with OpenAI's Codex, Anthropic's Claude Code, and Moonshot's Kimi Code, which independently developed and retained training approaches that raised success rates over repeated self-directed cycles.

title: AI Startup Midjourney Pivots to Health With Ultrasound Machine
blurb: Midjourney announced its first hardware project, an ultrasound machine, marking the image-generation startup's unexpected move into personal health and medical devices. The company shared the news at a San Francisco event, signaling a pivot beyond the consumer AI image tools that built its business.

title: Bringing more agent harnesses and frameworks to Cloudflare, starting with Flue
blurb: Cloudflare is bringing the production-hardening it built for Project Think into the Cloudflare Agents SDK, giving any agent harness a base layer of durable execution, dynamic code execution, a durable filesystem, and dynamic workflows. Flue, a new open-source framework from the team behind Astro and built on the Pi harness, is the first to ship on top of it, shipping its 1.0 Beta this week.

title: 80% of ChatGPT product recommendations change when search is enabled: Study
blurb: A study of 20,000 ChatGPT responses by Visibility Labs found that enabling search changed 80.2% of the chatbot's product recommendations, leaving only 19.8% overlap with results generated without web access. Even products recommended in every search-disabled run rarely carried over, with just 15.8% appearing once search was on, and search-enabled responses returned fewer products on average.

title: Explaining Attention with Program Synthesis
blurb: This paper approximates transformer attention heads with executable Python programs by prompting a language model to generate code that reproduces each head's attention patterns, then re-ranking by held-out fit. Fewer than 1,000 programs match heads in GPT-2, TinyLlama-1.1B, and Llama-3B above 75% IoU on TinyStories, and replacing 25% of heads with these surrogates raises perplexity by only 16% while preserving QA benchmark performance.

title: STARE: Surprisal-Guided Token-Level Advantage Reweighting for Policy Entropy Stability
blurb: STARE is a reinforcement learning method that stabilizes policy entropy in GRPO-style post-training by reweighting the effective advantages of entropy-critical tokens, identified through batch-internal surprisal quantiles and regulated by a target-entropy closed-loop gate. Tested across 1.5B to 32B models on short CoT, long CoT, and multi-turn tool use, it sustains stable training over thousands of steps and beats DAPO on AIME24 and AIME25 by 4% to 8% in average accuracy.

title: User as Engram: Internalizing Per-User Memory as Local Parametric Edits
blurb: This paper proposes User as Engram, which stores a user's facts as surgical edits to an Engram model's hash-keyed memory table while carrying reasoning skill in one shared adapter. The layered design matches per-user LoRA on direct recall while delivering 5.6x higher indirect-reasoning accuracy and a roughly 33,000x smaller memory footprint, since disjoint hash slots let many users' edits compose losslessly in one table.

title: CodeSentinel: A Three-Layer Defense Against Indirect Prompt Injection in Code Contexts
blurb: CodeSentinel is an inference-time sanitizer that defends code LLMs against indirect prompt injection hidden in comments, strings, identifiers, or decoy code retrieved from repositories and agent environments. It uses Tree-sitter to extract high-risk CST nodes, then applies syntax-guided pre-filtering, CST-guided Dynamic Min-K% scoring, and node perturbation analysis, hitting 0.80 average node-level F1 across six attack families and beating CodeGarrison, DePA, and KillBadCode.

title: Sumi: Open Uniform Diffusion Language Model from Scratch
blurb: Sumi is a fully open 7B uniform diffusion language model pretrained from scratch on 1.5T tokens, the first UDLM trained at this combined scale. It performs competitively with autoregressive models at comparable token budgets on knowledge, reasoning, and coding benchmarks while underperforming on commonsense tasks, and the authors released model weights, checkpoints, and the full training recipe including the data mixture.

title: SubQ 1.1 Small
blurb: SubQ released the model card for SubQ 1.1 Small, the second iteration of its Subquadratic Sparse Attention (SSA) model, which hits near-perfect needle-in-a-haystack retrieval up to 12M tokens. At 1M tokens, it requires 64.5x less compute than dense attention and runs 56x faster than FlashAttention-2, with broader 2M-to-12M-token models planned later this year.

title: Detecting Hidden ML Training With Zero-Overhead Telemetry
blurb: This paper tests whether GPU compute-governance monitoring can survive adversarial evasion, using only zero-overhead NVML telemetry that observes physical effects of computation without touching weights, training data, or hyperparameters. Across 5 rounds of monitor-evader iteration covering 20 evasion strategy families and 9 GPU models, the authors' classifier hit 98.2% binary accuracy at flagging training workloads and 43-87% against the hardest adversarially disguised cases.

title: Unlocking UK house-building with AI-accelerated planning
blurb: Google DeepMind partnered with the UK government to build a Gemini-powered prototype that helps planning officers process householder applications, aiming to cut decision times by 50%. Built alongside Google Cloud, Faculty, and councils in Barnet, Camden, and Dorset, the tool automates data extraction and case analysis, and the government plans to make it available to all councils nationally from 2027.

title: Anthropic's Fable shutdown is a big moment for open-source AI
blurb: Anthropic pulled access to its Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models for all customers late Friday to comply with a US export control directive citing national security authorities. The abrupt cutoff drove interest in downloadable open-source models that companies can run on their own infrastructure, with Chinese labs MiniMax and Zhipu surging on Monday as developers weighed the risk of single-vendor lock-in.

title: Anthropic got hit by export rules nobody understands
blurb: The Trump administration ordered Anthropic to cut off access to its Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models for all foreign nationals, including users and employees inside the US, citing national security authorities under an export control directive. Legal experts called the move unprecedented, noting that no model weights or source code change hands through a hosted chatbot, leaving unclear what Anthropic is supposedly exporting.

title: GLM-5.2: Built for Long-Horizon Tasks
blurb: Z.ai released GLM-5.2, an open-source model under an MIT license built for long-horizon coding agents, with a 1M-token context and an IndexShare architecture that cuts per-token FLOPs by 2.9x at full context length. On the FrontierSWE benchmark it trails Anthropic's Opus 4.8 by just 1% while edging out GPT-5.5, ranking as the strongest open-source model across three long-horizon coding benchmarks.

title: AI Use by the US Government
blurb: The OMB disclosed 3,611 active or planned AI use cases across the US federal government, a 70% increase over the list published in the final year of the Biden administration. The examples range from Palantir scanning HHS grant applications for ideological alignment to systems for inmate misconduct prediction, veterans crisis-line monitoring, and autonomous nuclear reactor control.

title: Midjourney Medical
blurb: Midjourney launched Midjourney Medical, a new health division built around a radiation-free full-body Ultrasonic CT scanner that uses 500,000 transducers to image the body in 60 seconds. The company plans to open its first scanning spa in San Francisco in 2027 and aims for a fleet of over 50,000 scanners handling a billion scans per month by 2031.

title: What’s New in the AI Platform: Agents for ML Engineering, Our Deep Learning Platform, and New Capabilities for Real-Time ML
blurb: Databricks announced new AI Platform capabilities at Data + AI Summit 2026, led by extending Genie Code to cover the full ML lifecycle from feature engineering to deployment. Genie Code selects and configures CPU or GPU infrastructure, logs runs to MLflow, and registers models to Unity Catalog, while the new AI Runtime trains large-scale deep learning models and Feature and Model Serving powers real-time ML.

title: OpenAnt: LLM-Powered Vulnerability Discovery Through Code Decomposition, Adversarial Verification, and Dynamic Testing
blurb: OpenAnt is an open-source system that combines static analysis with LLM reasoning in a multi-stage pipeline to find vulnerabilities in large codebases, cutting the analysis surface by up to 97% through reachability filtering and validating findings via adversarial verification and automatically generated exploits run in sandboxed containers. Evaluated on OpenSSL, WordPress, and Flowise, it surfaced previously unknown vulnerabilities while reducing false positives and keeping analysis cost manageable.

title: Sources: ByteDance has been Microsoft's biggest AI customer in recent years, largely using OpenAI models, and is on track to spend $1B+ a year on Azure services (Bloomberg)
blurb: ByteDance has been Microsoft's largest AI customer in recent years, primarily running OpenAI models through Azure, according to Bloomberg. The company is on track to spend over $1B a year on Azure services, underscoring how heavily TikTok's parent relies on Microsoft and OpenAI infrastructure despite its own AI ambitions.

title: Getting more from each token: How Copilot improves context handling and model routing
blurb: GitHub detailed two efficiency improvements to the Copilot harness in VS Code: prompt caching, which reuses model state for repeated prompt prefixes instead of recomputing them each request, and tool search, which loads tool definitions on demand rather than sending every full schema into context per turn. GitHub is also expanding Auto, Copilot's model routing, so the agent picks the model that fits each task without requiring developers to choose every time.

title: SpaceX to acquire vibe coding startup Cursor for $60B
blurb: SpaceX announced it will acquire Cursor, the developer of the popular vibe coding platform, for $60B in stock, with the deal expected to close by the end of the quarter. The acquisition follows an April partnership in which SpaceX supplied tens of thousands of GPUs to train Cursor's in-house Composer 2.5 model, and gives the rocket maker a platform with more than 1 million daily active users.

title: Midjourney goes from generating cat images to full-body ultrasound scans
blurb: Midjourney revealed its first hardware product, The Midjourney Scanner, a full-body ultrasound scanner built with Butterfly Network that uses a ring of thousands of transducers to capture 3D images of muscle, fat, bone, and organs in about 60 seconds. CEO David Holz said it aims for image quality comparable to MRI and is intended for routine preventative scanning, delivered through a planned San Francisco spa.

title: OpenAI Losses Increased Nearly 8X in 2025, with Spending Hitting $34B
blurb: OpenAI's losses grew nearly 8x in 2025 as total spending reached $34B, according to financial figures detailed in the report. Revenue growth failed to keep pace with the company's outlays on compute, talent, and infrastructure, widening the gap between what OpenAI takes in and what it spends to operate.

title: Nvidia reveals AI robots that taught themselves to install GPUs into motherboards — video shows robot ‘solve high-precision tasks like… installing GPUs all by itself’
blurb: Nvidia showcased ENPIRE, an open-source harness that let 8 Codex agents direct a fleet of robots to teach themselves high-precision tasks, including slotting a GPU into a motherboard, sorting metal pins, and cutting zip ties. The agents reset scenes, read papers, debate, and refine control code directly on the hardware, which Nvidia's Jim Fan framed as enabling AutoResearch in the physical world.

title: Nvidia researchers unveil ENPIRE, an agent harness framework that develops robotic self-improvement strategies for physical tasks with minimal human supervision (Jeremy Hsu/Ars Technica)
blurb: Nvidia researchers released ENPIRE, an agent harness framework that lets robots develop their own self-improvement strategies for physical tasks with minimal human supervision. The system generates and refines task-specific learning approaches autonomously, reducing the manual engineering typically required to train robots on new physical skills.

title: Ghost Vectors: Soft-Deleted Embeddings Remain Reconstructible in HNSW Vector Databases
blurb: This paper shows that soft-deleted embeddings in HNSW vector databases remain physically recoverable by reading raw index files, with a Vec2Text inversion model reconstructing 25.5% of exact names on a Wikipedia dataset and 100% of patient age and gender markers on synthetic health data. The authors propose Epoch Key Rotation, which encrypts vectors and discards the key on deletion, cutting observed PII recovery to 0% in 2.5ms per 500 records.

title: AWS enters the context layer race with a graph that learns from agents, not manual curation
blurb: AWS announced a context intelligence stack for AI agents, centered on AWS Context, a knowledge graph service that automatically maps relationships across existing data and refines itself based on how agents query it. The launch also includes general availability of Amazon S3 Annotations and a preview of skill assets in AWS Glue Data Catalog, with all metadata published in Apache Iceberg format and queries inheriting IAM and Lake Formation permissions.

title: Apple showcases its new developer AI tools in impressive 90-minute presentation
blurb: Apple shared a 90-minute WWDC26 developer session demonstrating its new AI tools, including a 20-minute segment where an entire app is built from a few prompts in Xcode 27. The presentation also covered the Apple Foundation Models, Core AI, and MLX frameworks, and closed by running the 1-trillion-parameter Kimi 2.6 model locally across four Mac Studios over RDMA-over-Thunderbolt.

title: SpaceX leapfrogs Amazon and briefly tops Microsoft in market value on Cursor acquisition news
blurb: SpaceX acquired AI coding startup Cursor for $60 billion, citing a goal to advance its frontier AI capabilities. The deal helped push SpaceX's market capitalization to roughly $2.94 trillion on Tuesday morning, briefly topping Microsoft and surpassing Amazon to become the world's fourth-most valuable public company less than a week after its $75 billion IPO.

title: Beyond Safe Data: Pretraining-Stage Alignment with Regular Safety Reflection
blurb: This paper introduces Safety Reflection Pretraining, an alignment method that inserts short safety reflections into pretraining corpora so self-monitoring is built directly into language modeling rather than relying on filtering or rewriting unsafe data. Experiments with 1.7B models on FineWeb-Edu improved safety classification accuracy and cut the success rates of inference-stage and finetuning attacks, with a synthetic benchmark, MedSafetyWorld, confirming the gains against behaviors generalized from safe data.

title: Rethinking Reward Supervision: Rubric-Conditioned Self-Distillation
blurb: This paper introduces Rubric-Conditioned Self-Distillation, a post-training method that conditions a teacher model on criterion-level rubrics to give token-level guidance on a student's own sampled reasoning trajectories. The two-stage pipeline first learns to generate task-specific rubrics, then trains a rubric-guided reasoner, beating GRPO by 1.0 points and OPSD by 0.9 points on average across science reasoning benchmarks.

title: Structured Inference with Large Language Gibbs
blurb: This paper introduces Large Language Gibbs, a scheme for structured probabilistic inference that uses an LLM's next-token conditional distributions as MCMC transition operators. Rather than sampling structured objects in a single autoregressive pass, the method iteratively resamples individual variables conditioned on the others, avoiding order-dependent biases and converging to a stationary distribution that reflects a compromise across all local conditionals.

title: US holds off blacklisting DeepSeek, more than 100 firms deemed security risks
blurb: The US declined to add China's DeepSeek to its trade blacklist, while designating more than 100 other firms as national security risks. The decision spares the AI startup from export restrictions that would cut its access to US technology, even as Washington broadened its crackdown on Chinese companies.

title: AMD acquires MEXT to add predictive memory optimization to its AI stack
blurb: AMD acquired memory optimization startup MEXT, adding predictive software that makes flash storage behave more like DRAM to its AI infrastructure portfolio. The technology uses AI to move frequently accessed data between flash and DRAM, expanding effective memory capacity as enterprises face DRAM supply growth below 16% and a forecasted 130% jump in memory prices by the end of 2026.

title: DreamReasoner-8B: Block-Size Curriculum Learning for Diffusion Reasoning Models
blurb: DreamReasoner-8B is an open-source block diffusion reasoning model trained with block-size curriculum learning, which gradually shifts training from fine-grained to coarse-grained block sizes. The authors found that large training block sizes cripple long chain-of-thought reasoning while small ones preserve it, and their curriculum closes that gap to match autoregressive models like Qwen3-8B on math and code benchmarks.

title: Evaluating Prompting-Based Defenses Against Domain-Camouflaged Injection Attacks
blurb: This paper evaluates five prompting-based defenses against domain-camouflaged injection attacks, which hide malicious instructions in retrieved content using domain-appropriate vocabulary to evade syntactic detectors. Across 3,510 trials on Claude Haiku, Llama 3.1 8B, and Gemini 2.0 Flash, paraphrasing retrieved content was the most consistent defense, cutting attack success by 55-84%, though financial deployments retained 26-33% baseline success that no defense fully eliminated.

title: Security Community Slams US Ban on Exporting Mythos, Fable
blurb: Dozens of security experts signed an open letter urging the US government to reverse export controls on Anthropic's Claude Mythos 5 and Fable 5 models, which the company suspended for all customers on June 12 to comply. The government cited national security concerns, reportedly tied to a suspected China-linked group accessing Mythos, a model said to be capable of discovering critical vulnerabilities and developing novel exploits.

title: World leaders want American AI. They just don’t want America to be able to turn it off.
blurb: At this week's G7 Summit, leaders including France's Macron and India's Modi warned that the U.S. could revoke their access to top American AI models at any time, following the Trump administration's block on Anthropic exporting its Mythos 5 and Fable 5 models. The leaders discussed a "trusted partners" scheme to grant non-U.S. nations access to advanced models from firms like Anthropic and OpenAI, bypassing U.S. restrictions.

title: What’s new in Genie Code at Data + AI Summit 2026
blurb: Databricks expanded Genie Code at Data + AI Summit 2026 with a full-page command center for complex, agentic data and ML work, alongside upgrades for production engineering and scheduled tasks. The redesign lets teams run multiple concurrent Genie Code threads across notebooks, SQL, Lakeflow pipelines, jobs, and Unity Catalog assets, tracking execution state and returning to each as results arrive.

title: Decoupling Search from Reasoning: A Vendor-Agnostic Grounding Architecture for LLM Agents
blurb: This paper introduces Decoupled Search Grounding (DSG), a vendor-agnostic architecture that moves retrieval out of the reasoning model and into an MCP-compatible gateway exposing provider routing, fallback, retrieval-depth control, and caching as first-class controls. Across five frontier models, DSG nearly matched native search accuracy on SimpleQA (86.1% vs. 87.7%) at 91% lower search cost, while cutting costs over 98% on an e-commerce workload.

title: SpaceX to acquire the AI coding startup Cursor for $60 billion
blurb: SpaceX agreed to acquire AI coding startup Cursor for $60B in stock, a deal expected to close in Q3 pending regulatory approval. The acquisition is meant to bolster SpaceX's frontier AI efforts against Anthropic and OpenAI after its xAI merger, though Cursor's category share has slipped from 41% in June 2025 to about 26% in May.

title: Anthropic ships major Claude Design overhaul with design system imports, code round-trips, and a fix for its token-burning problem
blurb: Anthropic shipped a major overhaul of Claude Design that adds design system imports from GitHub repos and design files, code round-trips, and a fix for the tool's heavy token consumption. Once a design system is imported, Claude builds with those components, checks its output against the system, and auto-corrects to keep generated work on brand and connected to existing enterprise tools.

title: AI demands more engineering discipline. Not less
blurb: The author argues that AI's ability to generate competent code raises the bar for engineering discipline rather than lowering it, drawing a parallel to the shift from handcrafted servers to immutable infrastructure. The piece dates the turning point to Opus 4.5 last November, which it frames as a tipping point rather than the cause, with agentic harnesses, tool use, and MCPs building usability throughout 2025. The takeaway is that code review and rigor matter more as generation gets cheaper, not less.

title: Microsoft Makes Big AI Inroads in China by Selling OpenAI Models
blurb: Microsoft has built a sizable business selling OpenAI models to Chinese companies through its Azure cloud, despite escalating US-China tensions over AI. The sales position Microsoft as a rare conduit for American frontier models in a market where most US AI firms face restrictions, even as Washington tightens export controls and Chinese rivals like DeepSeek and Alibaba expand domestically.

title: UBP2: Uncertainty-Balanced Preference Planning for Efficient Preference-based Reinforcement Learning
blurb: UBP2 is a model-based approach to preference-based reinforcement learning that actively directs exploration by jointly reasoning over uncertainty in the reward, dynamics, and value functions. Using ensembles to score candidate trajectories on a unified objective of expected reward, terminal value, and epistemic uncertainty, it achieves substantially higher sample efficiency than model-free and non-optimistic baselines on Meta-World, with sublinear regret guarantees.

title: Cybersecurity experts blast US government for restricting Anthropic’s AI models
blurb: Security leaders and researchers criticized a recent US export-control ban that restricts access to Anthropic's AI models, calling the move "dangerous." Critics argued the restriction could undermine cybersecurity defenses by limiting the tools available to legitimate practitioners while doing little to stop well-resourced adversaries.

title: From RAG to ontology: Databricks bets on context as the key to trusted AI agents
blurb: Databricks introduced Genie Ontology, a preview context layer that automatically extracts business definitions from enterprise data, dashboards, queries, and documents into a living graph that AI agents can query. It uses a PageRank-inspired ranking to weigh who created a definition, how widely it is used, and how recently it was updated, surfacing the most authoritative answer for each agent.

title: 'A signal of where power sits': Trump and world leaders joined by OpenAI, Anthropic, Google at G7
blurb: The CEOs of OpenAI, Anthropic, Google DeepMind, and roughly a dozen other AI firms joined Trump and G7 leaders at the summit in Evian to discuss frontier risks, infrastructure, and sovereignty. The gathering follows US export controls on Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models, which a CFR fellow said signals a shift in who holds power as heads of state increasingly need private-sector endorsement to make credible AI commitments.

title: SAGE: Stochastic Prompt Optimization via Agent-Guided Exploration
blurb: This paper introduces SPO, a framework that treats automatic prompt optimization as black-box search after finding that textual gradients don't behave like real gradients, and compares three strategies including SAGE, a multi-agent pipeline with diagnostic code execution. Across three benchmarks no single strategy dominates, but deployed on a mental-health chatbot, SAGE compounded eight noisy A/B cycles into a statistically robust gain in next-day retention.

title: What the Anthropic shutdown means for Europe
blurb: The US government ordered Anthropic to suspend access to its most advanced models for foreign nationals, prompting the company to cut off all customers, including those in the US, to ensure compliance. The episode marks the first activation of a US digital "kill switch" capable of severing European organizations from technologies they rely on for critical services, and has intensified European calls for sovereign AI infrastructure.

title: GraphPO: Graph-based Policy Optimization for Reasoning Models
blurb: GraphPO is a reinforcement learning framework that represents reasoning rollouts as a directed acyclic graph, with reasoning steps as edges and semantic states as nodes, merging equivalent paths to cut redundant exploration. Across three LLMs on reasoning and agentic search benchmarks, it outperforms chain- and tree-based RLVR baselines under the same token and response budgets while lowering advantage-estimation variance.

title: AWS unveils agent security, data access tools
blurb: AWS released new security and data access tools for AI agents, including capabilities to surface vulnerabilities in agent code faster and govern how agents reach enterprise data. The updates build on Anthropic's Mythos model and reflect AWS's push to make agentic deployments safer as customers move them into production environments.

title: Agentic Resource Discovery: Let agents search
blurb: Agentic Resource Discovery (ARD) is an open specification that lets agents search federated registries for tools, skills, and agents at runtime instead of hardcoding MCP server URLs or dumping every tool description into context. The spec defines an `ai-catalog.json` manifest and a `POST /search` endpoint for intent-based queries, and Hugging Face's Discover Tool wraps the Hub's semantic search to make thousands of running Spaces searchable as skills or MCP servers.

title: How to design AI agent loops: schedules, goals, and subagents in Claude Code and Codex
blurb: This podcast breaks down four ways to automate an LLM prompt into a loop: heartbeat, cron, hook, and goal-based, along with the five components any loop needs before production: work trees, skills, plugins, subagents, and state tracking. The author builds two live examples, a daily aging-PR reviewer in Claude Code that schedules itself and spawns subagents, and a weekly skills-identification loop in Codex that uses goal-based subagents to validate its own output.

title: US pulls the 'kill-switch' on Anthropic's Fable 5 AI models, sending global allies scrambling — European and Canadian leaders alarm allies over sudden export bans
blurb: The Trump administration blocked exports of Anthropic's Mythos 5 and Fable 5 models, cutting off allied governments from direct access to the frontier systems. European and Canadian leaders warned that the sudden restrictions may force them to build their own national AI alternatives to reduce dependence on US-controlled models.

title: Understanding and Mitigating Prompt Leaking Attacks in Real-World LLM-Based Applications
blurb: This paper measures prompt leaking across 1,200 real-world LLM applications on six commercial platforms and finds that over 80% leak their system prompts under adversarial queries, sometimes exposing third-party API keys. The authors trace the failure to attention drift and propose AREA, a soft-prompt defense that matches state-of-the-art leakage resistance while improving usability by over 33% and cutting optimization overhead nearly 3x.

title: CloakLM: Obfuscating GPU Memory Layout to Mitigate Model Ex-filtration for Serving
blurb: CloakLM is a software-only framework that obfuscates GPU memory layout to defend large models against inference-time exfiltration on shared or third-party accelerator infrastructure. It combines PCIe traffic shaping, inter- and intra-layer weight shuffling, and physical HBM page remapping, integrating with vLLM and PyTorch to resist PCIe snooping and HBM dump attacks with near-native performance on LLaMA and Qwen workloads.

title: PYPILINE: Malicious PyPI Package Detection via Suspicious API Knowledge and Agent Workflow
blurb: PYPILINE is a malicious PyPI package detector that pairs a suspicious-API knowledge base, built by static analysis of known-bad packages, with an agent workflow that performs semantic analysis and outputs interpretable maliciousness reports. It reports 96.7% precision, 99.6% recall, and a 98.1% F1-score, beating prior tools on precision by 5.7 to 24.2 percentage points.

title: World model maker Odyssey nabs $1.45B valuation backed by Amazon and other big names
blurb: Odyssey, a world model startup founded by self-driving veterans Oliver Cameron and Jeff Hawke, raised a $310M Series B at a $1.45B valuation led by Natural Capital, with Amazon, AMD Ventures, and GV participating. As part of the round, Odyssey named AWS its preferred cloud provider and will optimize its models, which generate interactive video from text prompts, to run on AWS Trainium chips.

title: Google bets on Gemini to reinvent the smart home speaker
blurb: Google introduced the $99.99 Google Home Speaker, its first stand-alone smart speaker since the 2020 Nest Audio and the first built specifically for Gemini. The device handles natural-language and multistep commands, supports mid-sentence corrections and follow-up questions, and ships with 10 new voices, while more advanced features like free-flowing Gemini Live chats sit behind a $10-per-month Home Premium plan.

title: Midjourney unveils its first hardware product, the Midjourney Scanner, an ultrasound-based full-body scanner; it is unclear how AI fits into the medical effort (Richard Lawler/The Verge)
blurb: Midjourney introduced its first hardware product, the Midjourney Scanner, an ultrasound-based full-body scanner that marks the image-generation startup's move into medical devices. The company has not explained how AI factors into the effort, leaving the scanner's connection to its core text-to-image and video models unclear.

title: Opinion | Why Does Trump Hate Anthropic?
blurb: The author argues that the Trump administration's de facto ban on Anthropic's Fable 5 model sets a dangerous precedent for how Washington treats AI companies it disfavors. The piece frames the move as politically motivated rather than security-driven, warning that punishing a leading US lab over policy disagreements risks weakening the country's position against China in the AI race.

title: A survey of US adults: 49% reported using chatbots in 2026, up from 33% in 2024, 24% said that they use chatbots on a daily basis, and 44% said they use ChatGPT (Pew Research Center)
blurb: A Pew Research Center survey found that 49% of US adults reported using chatbots in 2026, up from 33% in 2024, with 24% saying they use them daily. ChatGPT remained the most common tool at 44% adoption, signaling that conversational AI has moved from early-adopter novelty toward routine use across a broad share of the population.

title: Anthropic Ban Forces Investor Rethink of Political Risk
blurb: A ban involving Anthropic has pushed investors to weigh political risk as a serious threat to the AI stock rally, alongside stretched valuations and heavy capital spending. The episode signals that government action can disrupt even the strongest AI names, adding a regulatory and policy variable that markets had largely overlooked during the sector's run.

title: Amazon has lagged OpenAI and Anthropic, but AI chief sees path to catch up in 'coming year'
blurb: Amazon AI executive Peter DeSantis told CNBC the company aims to compete with OpenAI and Anthropic on frontier models within the "coming year," acknowledging its models haven't matched the leading labs on the most demanding workloads. Amazon now has about 50,000 customers for Nova2, its latest model released in December, and is leaning on custom Trainium and Graviton chips to close the gap.

title: Star Google Researcher Joins OpenAI in Coup for ChatGPT Creator
blurb: A prominent Google researcher is leaving for OpenAI, marking a setback for Alphabet in the multibillion-dollar race to build the most capable AI models. The departure adds to the intensifying talent competition between the two labs, where senior researchers have become a primary lever in the contest to ship frontier systems.

title: Claude Design will now stick to your brand guidelines instead of generic AI mockups
blurb: Anthropic updated Claude Design, its visual creation tool, to import design systems from GitHub repositories, design files, or uploaded assets and check its output against those approved components before showing results. The release also adds a /design-sync handoff to Claude Code, a rebuilt canvas editor, PDF and PowerPoint export, and integrations with Adobe, Canva, Figma rivals like Miro, Replit, Vercel, and Wix.

title: Microsoft launches Copilot Cowork globally for Microsoft 365
blurb: Microsoft made Copilot Cowork generally available worldwide for Microsoft 365 Copilot customers, following a three-month preview that drew adoption from over half the Fortune 500. The cloud-hosted agentic system executes long-running, multi-tool tasks end-to-end and offers a multi-model architecture including Anthropic Opus 4.8, Sonnet 4.6, GPT 5.5, and the upcoming lower-cost Cowork 1 model, billed via usage-based Copilot Credits.

title: Server-Side Tools Are Now Available for DigitalOcean Inference Engine
blurb: DigitalOcean released Server-Side Tools in Public Preview for its Inference Engine, letting a model call web search, web fetch, Knowledge Bases, MCP servers, and supported Anthropic and OpenAI tools from inside a single inference request. The tools work with an existing Model Access Key, with no separate tool infrastructure or orchestration layer to operate, and web search and fetch are powered by Exa under usage-based pricing.

title: Data Intelligence Agents: Interpreting, Modeling, and Querying Enterprise Data via Autonomous Coding Agents
blurb: Data Intelligence Agents (DIA) is a production system of three agents that treats autonomous coding agents as a first-class abstraction, generating, executing, validating, and repairing concrete artifacts rather than emitting text. Its Query Generator matched or surpassed the best published results across seven SQL benchmarks spanning four task categories and four dialects in fully autonomous mode.

title: GPT‑NL: a sovereign language model for the Netherlands
blurb: TNO, together with SURF and the Netherlands Forensic Institute, is building GPT-NL, an independent Dutch language model trained entirely from scratch to strengthen the digital autonomy of the Netherlands and Europe. The project publishes its source code as open source and releases model weights under a controlled license, avoiding dependency on non-European providers and inherited copyright or data-provenance risks from existing models.

title: Native Active Perception as Reasoning for Omni-Modal Understanding
blurb: OmniAgent is an omni-modal video understanding agent that treats the task as a POMDP-based Observation-Thought-Action loop, selectively distilling audio-visual cues into persistent textual memory instead of processing every frame uniformly. Trained with agentic supervised fine-tuning and reinforcement learning, the 7B model reaches state-of-the-art results among open-source models across ten benchmarks, beating the 10x larger Qwen2.5-VL-72B on LVBench (50.5% vs. 47.3%).

title: REVES: REvision and VErification--Augmented Training for Test-Time Scaling
blurb: REVES is a two-stage training framework that improves test-time scaling by converting the intermediate "near-miss" answers in successful recovery trajectories into decoupled revision and verification prompts, concentrating learning on both answer correction and error identification. On LiveCodeBench it gains 6.5 points over an RL baseline and 4.0 over standard multi-turn training, and it matches the prior circle-packing SOTA with a 4B base model and far fewer rollouts.

title: Image Prompt Reconstruction Attacks on Distributed MLLM Inference Frameworks
blurb: This paper presents the first study of image reconstruction attacks against distributed MLLM inference frameworks, where intermediate embeddings shared across consumer devices can leak private image prompts. The authors introduce two passive black-box attacks, MPAA for pixel-level reconstruction and IEDA for semantic reconstruction, achieving 100% embedding extraction accuracy and strong recovery across Gemma 3, Phi 4 Multimodal, Qwen 2.5 VL, and Llama 4 Scout.

title: [AINews] Midjourney Medical: scan your organs like you step on a scale
blurb: Midjourney announced the Midjourney Scanner, a full-body ultrasonic CT system that David Holz called the first new whole-body medical imaging modality in 50 years. The Gen 1 prototype uses 358,000 ultrasonic elements across a 70cm ring to capture sub-millimeter tissue detail without radiation, though Midjourney says the current images involve no AI and the device remains an unproven prototype.

title: Code-Augur: Agentic Vulnerability Detection via Specification Inference
blurb: Code-Augur is an agentic vulnerability detection harness that makes an LLM agent's tacit security assumptions explicit as in-source assertions, then runs a guided fuzzer to falsify them. When the fuzzer trips an assertion, it surfaces either a real vulnerability or a flawed specification to refine, and the authors report 22 new vulnerabilities found in major open-source projects using widely available models like Sonnet and DeepSeek.

title: Estonia intends to recognize AI agents with digital IDs
blurb: Estonia plans to issue digital IDs to AI agents, extending its national e-identity system to autonomous software acting on behalf of citizens and businesses. The move would give agents verifiable identities for signing documents and accessing services, addressing the gap in access models built for human users rather than non-human identities operating independently.

title: CEOs of Anthropic and Google DeepMind call for U.S.-led AI coalition in meeting at G7
blurb: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei and Google DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis called for a U.S.-led international coalition to set AI rules and standards at a closed-door G7 lunch attended by President Trump, OpenAI's Sam Altman, and other heads of state. Amodei proposed structured access to frontier models and chip trade that excludes China, while the meeting followed U.S. export controls that forced Anthropic to disable its Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models.

title: Anthropic asked for regulation. Washington went much further
blurb: Anthropic received a federal export control directive ordering it to suspend access to its newest Claude models, Fable 5 and Mythos 5, for any foreign national, days after CEO Dario Amodei published an essay calling for binding AI regulation. The administration cited unspecified "national security authorities," and Anthropic disputed the finding as a "misunderstanding," sending senior staff to Washington to resolve the disruption.

title: Sources: JPMorgan Chase has stopped its staff in Hong Kong from accessing Anthropic's AI models, following a similar move by rival Goldman Sachs (Financial Times)
blurb: JPMorgan Chase has barred its Hong Kong staff from using Anthropic's AI models, following a similar restriction by rival Goldman Sachs. The moves point to mounting compliance and data-security concerns among major banks over deploying third-party AI tools in markets with stricter regulatory and cross-border data rules.

title: Announcing Web Search on Amazon Bedrock AgentCore: Ground your AI agents in current, accurate web knowledge
blurb: AWS made Web Search on Amazon Bedrock AgentCore generally available, a managed tool that grounds agent responses in cited web results with zero data egress from the customer's AWS environment. It connects through the AgentCore Gateway over MCP and combines Amazon's web index with structured Knowledge Graph data, returning snippets, source URLs, titles, and publication dates the model can reason over.

title: Introducing Amazon Bedrock Managed Knowledge Base for faster, more accurate enterprise AI applications
blurb: Amazon Bedrock Managed Knowledge Base is a new service that abstracts the storage, retrieval, embeddings, re-ranking, and model selection behind RAG pipelines into a single managed primitive. It ships with six native data connectors that pull enterprise data and permissions from SaaS applications, and defaults to managed embeddings, re-ranker, and foundation models so developers can build knowledge bases without assembling the infrastructure themselves.

title: SpaceX's all-stock Cursor deal illustrates the power of public markets, which could help SpaceX narrow the gap with Anthropic and OpenAI through acquisitions (New York Times)
blurb: SpaceX agreed to acquire AI coding startup Cursor in an all-stock deal, using its richly valued private shares as currency. The structure shows how access to public-market-like valuations could let SpaceX fund further acquisitions and narrow the gap with frontier labs like Anthropic and OpenAI.

title: Epic Games just took a big step toward AI-built games with Unreal Engine 5.8
blurb: Epic Games released Unreal Engine 5.8, the final major UE5 release, which ships an experimental Model Context Protocol plugin that connects any LLM to core engine systems including blueprints, assets, levels, and meshes. Epic demonstrated Anthropic's Claude Code arranging scene objects and matching lighting to reference images, and framed the plugin as groundwork for UE6, where LLM integration is set to become central to the creation pipeline.

title: A Colombian AI startup wants to assist half of Latin America’s doctors. Andreessen Horowitz just backed it.
blurb: Telepatia, a Colombian AI clinical assistant, raised $33M in a Series A led by Andreessen Horowitz, bringing total funding to $42M and targeting half of Latin America's 1.9M doctors by the end of 2027. The tool transcribes consultations in real time, flags potential drug interactions and errors, and reportedly saves physicians 1.7 hours per day across more than 14M patients in five countries.

title: DeepSeek was set to be added to US Entity List for supporting China’s military and intelligence operations, report claims — White House holds off to avoid escalating tensions with China
blurb: A new report claims the White House blocked adding DeepSeek and memory chipmaker CXMT to the US Entity List, despite both being flagged for supporting China's military and intelligence operations. The administration held off on the export restrictions to avoid escalating trade tensions with Beijing.

title: Midjourney, the AI image generator, is developing a full-body ultrasonic scanner
blurb: Midjourney, known for its text-to-image generator, announced Midjourney Medical and a first hardware product, the Scanner, a full-body ultrasonic machine built with Butterfly Network that aims to produce MRI-quality 3D body maps in under 60 seconds. The company plans to operate spa locations housing the scanners, and will spend the next 12 months running research trials and refining the algorithms and second-generation hardware.

title: ICYMI: ZAI launches GLM-5.2 open model with 1M context
blurb: Z.ai released GLM-5.2, an open flagship coding model with a 1M-token context window and up to 128K output tokens, built for long-horizon agent tasks like full-repository implementation, debugging, and refactoring. The 744B-parameter model (40B active) scores 81.0 on Terminal-Bench 2.1 and 62.1 on SWE-bench Pro, and is available to GLM Coding Plan users inside agents like Claude Code, OpenClaw, and Cline.

title: TxBench-PP: Analyzing AI Agent Performance on Small-Molecule Preclinical Pharmacology
blurb: TxBench-PP is a verifiable benchmark testing whether AI agents can recover accurate preclinical pharmacology conclusions from real-world small-molecule assay data rather than facts memorized from literature. Across 16 model-harness configurations spanning 11 models and 4,800 trajectories, no system reliably recovered preclinical decisions, with the strongest setup, Claude Opus 4.8 with Pi, passing just 59.3% of endpoint attempts.

title: Enhancing Decision-Making with Large Language Models through Multi-Agent Fictitious Play
blurb: This paper introduces Multi-Agent Fictitious Play (MAFP), a multi-agent paradigm that represents each stakeholder's stance as a separate LLM agent and treats decision-making as an equilibrium-seeking process. Built on the game-theoretic principle of fictitious play, agents iteratively best-respond to the empirical mix of others' past decisions, outperforming single-round and multi-round baselines on tournament strength and robustness in competitive scenarios.

title: Does VLA Even Know the Basics? Measuring Commonsense and World Knowledge Retention in Vision-Language-Action Models
blurb: This paper introduces Act2Answer, a protocol that evaluates how much commonsense and factual knowledge Vision-Language-Action models retain after fine-tuning by requiring agents to answer questions through a single object-placement action. Across 7 VLA models and 9 VLM baselines, the authors find that VLAs hold up on simple concepts but lose ground on richer semantic categories relative to their source VLMs, with answer-relevant signals peaking in middle layers and fading in upper ones.

title: Essential Subspace Merging for Multi-Task Learning
blurb: This paper introduces Essential Subspace Merging (ESM), a training-free method for combining multiple models fine-tuned from the same checkpoint into one multi-task model by orthogonalizing and fusing only the principal directions that carry most task-relevant energy. The authors also extend it to ESM++, a dynamic variant that decomposes residuals into low-rank experts and routes to the most relevant one at inference, reducing inter-task interference across multiple task sets and model scales.

title: Diffusion-Proof: Recipe for Formal Theorem Proving Beyond Auto-Regressive Generation
blurb: Diffusion-Proof is the first framework to train and apply diffusion LLMs to formal theorem proving, pairing dLLM-Prover-7B for whole-proof generation with dLLM-Corrector-7B, a block-diffusion model that uses bidirectional in-filling for local proof correction. It beats an auto-regressive baseline trained on the same data by 1.61% on ProofNet-Test and 6.14% on MiniF2F-Test, and solved one IMO problem that DeepSeek-Prover-V2-7B could not.

title: Leadership as Coordination Control: Behavioral Signatures and the Recovery-Advantage Boundary in Multi-Agent LLM Teams
blurb: This paper studies when process-level coordination control helps multi-agent LLM teams, operationalizing transactional, transformational, and situational leadership as explicit controllers over a shared action vocabulary of explore, revise, accept, and synthesize. Across four task regimes and three open-weight model families no controller dominates on accuracy, with gains appearing only where the round-0 majority is unreliable yet the task remains recoverable.

title: Lifecycle-Aware Dynamic Analysis for Secure ML Model Execution
blurb: This paper introduces Moat, a dynamic lifecycle-aware approach for securing ML model execution that monitors how a model interacts with the host system rather than relying on static format-specific rules or known attack signatures. Evaluated through its Re-Moat implementation across 77,974 Hugging Face artifacts, 31 CVE proofs-of-concept, and 334 models, it detected every tested attack class while maintaining a near-zero false-positive rate.

title: Stealthy World Model Manipulation via Data Poisoning
blurb: This paper introduces SWAAP, a two-stage data poisoning framework that manipulates learned world models by corrupting only a fraction of fine-tuning transition targets. SWAAP uses first-order bilevel optimization to identify a harmful target model, then realizes it through stealth-constrained gradient matching, causing substantial performance degradation across continuous-control tasks while evading residual, CUSUM, and TRIM-style defenses.

title: TRAP: Benchmark for Task-completion and Resistance to Active Privacy-extraction
blurb: This paper introduces TRAP, a benchmark that measures whether AI agents can use private fields like passport numbers to complete tasks without leaking them to natural-language extraction attacks. Across 22 frontier and open-source models all leaked non-trivially, instruction-following correlated with higher leakage, and the authors prove no prompt-based defense can reach zero leakage, proposing instead to replace private fields with hash keys before they reach the model.

title: SWE-Future: Forecast-Conditioned Data Synthesis for Future-Oriented Software Engineering Agents
blurb: SWE-Future is a forecast-conditioned data synthesis method that builds coding-agent benchmarks from pre-snapshot repository evidence to predict future feature, bugfix, and refactor tasks rather than replaying public GitHub pull requests. Across an 80-repository study the forecaster hit 58.1% future-work relevance, and the authors used those validated forecasts to synthesize a 200-task dataset spanning 61 repositories while reducing contamination from historical PR replay.

title: CEO-Bench: Can Agents Play the Long Game?
blurb: CEO-Bench is a benchmark that tests agents on long-horizon, adaptive decision-making by simulating the operation of a fictional startup for 500 days through a programmable Python interface, requiring agents to manage pricing, marketing, and budgeting while analyzing noisy, interconnected business databases. Only Claude Opus 4.8 and GPT-5.5 finished above the $1M starting balance, and neither consistently turned a profit.

title: ChatGPT's image generator can be manipulated to produce violent, sexual content
blurb: Mindgard research found that ChatGPT's image generator can be manipulated into producing violent and sexually explicit content, including depictions of death and sexual violence, without users directly requesting it. The findings follow earlier reports that OpenAI's safety measures against AI-generated nudes remained bypassable, raising questions about why these models are trained on such imagery in the first place.

title: Only 16 percent of Americans think AI will have a positive impact on society, a new study shows
blurb: A new Pew Research study found that only 16% of Americans believe AI will have a positive impact on society over the next 20 years, while around 40% expect a negative one. Skepticism runs deep elsewhere too, with 67% doubting the government will meaningfully regulate AI and 59% distrusting companies to develop it safely, even as a quarter of Americans now use chatbots daily.

title: OpenAI readies ChatGPT for Science subscription plan
blurb: OpenAI appears to be preparing a dedicated "ChatGPT for Science" subscription aimed at universities, national laboratories, and corporate R&D groups working in biology, chemistry, physics, and materials. References to the plan sit alongside the company's existing vertical packages, and they hint at a science-oriented model built on the OpenAI for Science team's work, though no pricing, eligibility, or timeline has been confirmed.

title: The IPO Onslaught Is Forcing Bankers to Pick Teams
blurb: Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley are forming separate internal teams to handle the IPOs of OpenAI and Anthropic, walling off the deals to prevent information sharing between the rival accounts. The arrangement reflects the unusual position of advising two competing frontier labs heading toward public listings at the same time.

title: Uncle Sam bets $500M that Alphabet spinoff's AI can dig up new semiconductor materials
blurb: The US government committed $500M to an Alphabet spinoff whose AI tools search for new semiconductor materials, aiming to strengthen domestic chip supply chains. The funding backs efforts to use machine learning to accelerate the discovery and simulation of novel materials, a process that traditionally takes years of lab experimentation.

title: Local Qwen isn't a worse Opus, it's a different tool
blurb: The author argues that local Qwen models aren't a cheaper substitute for Opus but a distinct tool with a different cost-benefit profile, drawing on real numbers from running a bootstrapped software business. An RTX 6000 Pro card paid for itself within two or three months on his specific Go and infrastructure workloads, but Qwen still can't be trusted unsupervised. Its worst failure modes, infinite loops and hallucination, surface most once quantized down to fit a consumer GPU.

title: Ineffable Intelligence and Google Cloud partner on new frontier AI lab
blurb: Ineffable Intelligence selected Google Cloud as the preferred cloud partner for its new London-based frontier AI lab, with Google providing infrastructure tuned for reinforcement learning workloads. The deal follows Ineffable's $1.1B seed round, the largest in European history, and supports the lab's goal of building a "superlearner" that generates and learns from experience in real time rather than static datasets.

title: Odyssey raises $310M at $1.45B valuation to transform AI model simulation
blurb: Odyssey, an AI lab building world models, raised $310M in Series B funding at a $1.45B valuation, led by Natural Capital with participation from Amazon, AMD Ventures, and GV. The round came alongside a deal making AWS its preferred cloud provider, giving Odyssey access to Trainium silicon to train models that simulate physics and causality for robotics and autonomous systems.

title: A Technical Taxonomy of LLM Agent Communication Protocols
blurb: This paper develops a technical taxonomy for classifying LLM agent communication protocols, analyzing nine actively maintained open-source protocols across five dimensions: counterparty, payload, interaction state, discovery mechanism, and schema flexibility. The authors find that all sampled agent-to-agent protocols pair hybrid payloads with session-state persistence, decentralized discovery remains rare, and the field is likely to converge on a federated, layered protocol stack rather than a single dominant standard.

title: Pareto Q-Learning with Reward Machines
blurb: This paper introduces Pareto Q-Learning with Reward Machines (PQLRM), a multi-objective reinforcement learning algorithm for tasks whose reward structure is specified by reward machines. By pairing Pareto Q-Learning's vector-valued Q-estimates with QRM's exploitation of factored automaton structure, PQLRM stays sample-efficient under non-Markovian rewards, converges faster than a naive PQL baseline on the cross-product MDP, and synthesizes Pareto-optimal policies that QRM cannot.

title: Leak confirms OpenAI is testing a ChatGPT for Science subscription
blurb: A leak from ChatGPT's web build shows OpenAI is testing "ChatGPT for Science," a subscription tier likely restricted to verified universities and research institutions. The tier appears to extend specialized scientific grounding beyond OpenAI's partner-only GPT-Rosalind model, with no launch date set but an announcement expected within weeks.

title: Agentic coding deserves more than a chat box bolted onto VS Code
blurb: Polypore is an open-source agentic desktop IDE built on Tauri 2 and React that treats the AI agent as the primary actor rather than a chat panel bolted onto an editor, with every surface a sandboxed, dockable panel behind a shared contract. A Node MCP sidecar gives agents direct IDE control through 22-plus tools, third-party plugins run as sandboxed iframes, and secrets stay in the OS keyring so the model never sees plaintext.

title: Smelly config files will make your agents waste tokens, researchers warn
blurb: Researchers found that "smelly" agent configuration files, those with poor structure, redundancy, or unclear instructions, cause AI agents to consume significantly more tokens completing the same tasks. The study identifies common config anti-patterns that inflate context and degrade performance, arguing that treating agent configs with the same rigor as production code can cut waste and improve reliability.

title: Launch HN: Adam (YC W25) – Open-Source AI CAD
blurb: Adam, a YC W25 startup, released CADAM, an open-source tool that turns natural-language prompts and images into fully parametric OpenSCAD models directly in the browser. It runs locally via WebAssembly, ships with the BOSL, BOSL2, and MCAD libraries, and exports finished models as .STL, .SCAD, or .DXF files.

title: Why cloud native belongs at the heart of agentic AI: Lessons from building a multi-agent security platform on Kubernetes
blurb: The author argues that cloud native infrastructure is the right foundation for production agentic AI, drawing on a multi-agent security platform being built at Orange Innovation on Kubernetes. Each agent runs as its own Deployment with isolated resource limits and identity, coordinated via the A2A protocol and LangGraph, with Falco and eBPF feeding syscall events through Kafka into an anomaly model that pre-filters before the LLM-driven agents act.

title: Building an open ecosystem for AI governance with Unity AI Gateway
blurb: Databricks announced the Unity AI Gateway partner ecosystem at Data + AI Summit 2026, extending its Unity Catalog governance to the runtime interactions between models, agents, MCP servers, and AI tools. The launch adds integrations across AI security, identity, and observability, letting enterprises plug providers like CrowdStrike, Okta, Palo Alto Networks, and Zscaler into governed AI workflows that evaluate prompts, responses, and tool calls in real time.

title: Only half of US datacenter capacity planned for 2026 is actually  under construction
blurb: A report found that only about half of the US datacenter capacity slated to come online in 2026 is currently under construction, suggesting a wide gap between announced buildout plans and physical reality. The shortfall raises questions about whether projected AI compute supply will materialize on schedule, even as demand forecasts and capital commitments continue to climb.

title: AWS hypes continuous agentic DevOps, puts Kiro in your pocket
blurb: AWS expanded Bedrock AgentCore with continuous agentic DevOps features and added mobile access to its Kiro coding agent.

title: Robotic teleoperation data startup XDOF launches with $70M in funding
blurb: XDOF launched with $70M in funding from Thrive Capital, Spark Capital, Andreessen Horowitz, Lux Capital, and WndrCo to build the data pipelines, collection tools, and annotation systems needed to train robots for real-world tasks. Alongside the round, the company released ABC-130K, which it calls the world's largest open-source bimanual robot manipulation dataset.

title: Build and Deploy a Remote MCP Server to GKE in 30 Minutes
blurb: This Google Cloud guide walks through deploying a remote MCP server to Google Kubernetes Engine using the Streamable HTTP transport, which lets a single stateless server handle multiple client connections over HTTP POST and GET. Running it on GKE Autopilot adds horizontal scaling for traffic spikes, centralized access for teams instead of redundant local servers, and encrypted-only traffic enforced through the Kubernetes Gateway API with SSL certificates.

title: HANSEL: Extracting Breadcrumbs from Web Agent Trajectories for Interactive Verification
blurb: HANSEL is a system that extracts interactive, verifiable evidence from AI web-agent trajectories, turning correctness-checking from passive log-reading into navigable evidence views that preserve page state like filters, queries, and scroll positions. On 45 tasks from AssistantBench and Online-Mind2Web it hit 83.7% precision and 88.8% recall at identifying evidence pages while cutting trajectory volume by 61.6%, and flags answers it cannot trace to any visited page.

title: Tether is shipping TurboQuant KV-cache quantization with Vulkan support into its QVAC SDK
blurb: Tether shipped TurboQuant, Google's KV-cache compression algorithm, into v0.12.0 of its QVAC SDK with Vulkan support, making it the first team to bring the technique to a publicly available local AI model. The integration lets developers run inference through qvac-fabric-llm.cpp with up to 5x less VRAM regardless of context size and negligible precision loss.

title: U.S. gov't asks court to dismiss NAACP lawsuit against Elon Musk's xAI over use of unpermitted gas turbines — DOJ says Grok model running at Colossus 2 ‘supports mission-critical operations’
blurb: The DOJ filed a memorandum urging a court to dismiss the NAACP's lawsuit against Elon Musk's xAI over unpermitted natural gas turbines running its Colossus 2 data center in Memphis. Federal lawyers argued that shutting down the turbines threatens national security because Grok is one of only four AI models used to support mission-critical military operations across Secret and Top-Secret networks.

title: AI material discovery startup CuspAI reportedly raising $400M round
blurb: CuspAI is reportedly raising $400M at a $2.6B valuation, more than five times its prior worth, with participation from Bezos Expeditions and Kleiner Perkins. The UK startup runs an AI platform that generates material designs and tests them in simulation, with early customers including ASML, Meta, and Hyundai.

title: Mistral CEO pitches open source AI days after Anthropic ban: ‘We exist outside of state control’
blurb: Mistral CEO Arthur Mensch pitched the company's open-source models as a sovereign alternative days after the US government ordered Anthropic to cut access to two of its models for foreign nationals. The Paris-based firm, which has raised €3.5bn to date and is reportedly in talks for another €3bn at a roughly €20bn valuation, is positioning open weights and on-premise control as its edge in Europe.

title: ECB’s Lagarde says AI could trigger financial crises and calls for Cold War-style non-proliferation governance
blurb: ECB President Christine Lagarde warned that AI could trigger financial crises more damaging than job losses, and called for global governance modeled on Cold War nuclear non-proliferation treaties. The ECB has stress-tested 109 banks against an AI-powered cyberattack scenario, and Lagarde said she will write to bank CEOs demanding serious investment in resilience.

title: Barcelona-based NeuralTrust raises €17.2 million to secure and govern enterprise AI agents
blurb: NeuralTrust, a Barcelona-based startup that secures and governs enterprise AI agents, raised €17.2M ($20M) in a seed round led by Alstin Capital, which it calls the largest cybersecurity seed financing by an EU company to date. The platform lets enterprises like banks and airlines inventory every AI agent running across their systems, manage its permissions, and intervene when one is duped into leaking data or taking unauthorized actions.

title: Vercel launches a new framework and enterprise controls for agentic AI infrastructure
blurb: Vercel introduced a set of agentic AI infrastructure products at its Ship conference, including Vercel Services for single-commit full-stack deployments, the Agent Stack tooling bundle, eve open-source agent framework, and enterprise controls. The push follows a sharp shift on its platform, where agent-triggered commits grew from under 3% to more than half and AI Gateway token volume rose from 2 million to 20 million this year.

title: CuspAI to hit $2.6bn valuation with backing from Jeff Bezos, reports say
blurb: CuspAI is raising $400M at a $2.6B valuation, with backing from Jeff Bezos's family office Bezos Expeditions and Kleiner Perkins, according to a Financial Times report. The Cambridge-based startup, which runs AI models that propose new molecules and predict their real-world performance for uses like sustainable energy and AI compute, would more than quadruple the $100M valuation from its September raise.

title: After taking Nvidia’s money, Odyssey raises $310M and bets on Amazon and AMD instead
blurb: Odyssey raised $310M at a $1.45B valuation in a round led by Natural Capital, with Amazon, AMD Ventures, GV, EQT, and In-Q-Tel participating. The deal names AWS as the world-model lab's preferred cloud provider and supplies it with Amazon's Trainium chips, a pointed pivot four months after Nvidia's venture arm backed Odyssey's Series A.

title: This French startup’s AI is already calling shots on battlefields. Now, it’s raised €32M to scale
blurb: Comand AI, a Paris startup whose Prevail command-and-control platform is already deployed with operational units in Ukraine, France, and Germany, raised €32M in a Series A led by Blossom Capital. Swedish defense group Saab joined as a strategic backer, signaling that incumbent defense firms are buying into AI-native command software rather than building it, as rivals Helsing and Anduril command $18B and $61B valuations.

title: Mechanism-Guided Selective Unlearning for RLVR-Induced Reasoning
blurb: This paper introduces MAST (Mechanism-Aligned Selective Targeting), a method for unlearning RLVR-induced reasoning that ranks attention-projection tensors by off-principal energy, update magnitude, and forget-gradient coupling, then updates only the top-ranked subset. On Qwen2.5-Math-1.5B, MAST achieved statistically significant target forgetting (MATH 45/150 to 37/150, McNemar p=0.0078) while preserving GSM8K and MATH retain accuracy, whereas full-parameter gradient ascent forgot only by damaging retained performance.

title: Optimal scenario design for climate emulation
blurb: This paper shows that the low structural diversity of standard climate scenarios caps the predictive skill of machine-learning climate emulators. The authors use a differentiable Simple Climate Model to optimize training scenarios for emulator loss, and a single optimized scenario outperforms an emulator trained on six standard ScenarioMIP pathways while isolating distinct forcing behaviors like greenhouse gases versus aerosols.

title: Learning User Simulators with Turing Rewards
